title: Somatosensory evoked potentials in the human spinal cord to mixed nerve stimulation
description: This dataset comprises simultaneous multimodal neurophysiological recordings from 40 participants, including electroencephalography (EEG), electrospinography (ESG), electroneurography (ENG), and electromyography (EMG), acquired during resting state and peripheral nerve stimulation conditions. The study investigates somatosensory evoked potentials in the human spinal cord elicited by mixed median and tibial nerve stimulation, with concurrent monitoring of electrocardiographic and respiratory signals. Data are organized according to the Brain Imaging Data Structure specification.
methods_description: Simultaneous EEG-ESG-ENG-EMG recordings were acquired from 40 participants under four conditions: resting state with eyes open, mixed median nerve stimulation, mixed tibial nerve stimulation, and alternating mixed median or tibial nerve stimulation. Recordings included electrocardiographic and respiratory signals. EEG channel Fz was referenced to both the right mastoid (standard EEG reference) and the 6th thoracic vertebra (ESG reference).
